title: lagascea aurea
intro: Lagascea aurea, also known as Golden Ragwort, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and pain, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Golden Ragwort can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or topical salve, with its herbal preparations often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and other inflammatory disorders.
